About
Talk to an engineer

Electrical Signature Analysis

Every drivetrain fault leaves a trace in your electrical data.

SAM4 reads that trace from the motor control cabinet to detect bearing wear, cavitation, winding defects, and misalignment, weeks to months before failure. No sensors on the machine. Every alert validated by a Samotics reliability engineer before it reaches your team.

10,000+ assets monitored
90%+ detection accuracy
1.6% false alert rate
See How It Works Talk to an engineer

Why SAM4

Covers the assets sensors can't reach

Submersible pumps, sealed compressors, ATEX zones, remote sites. SAM4 monitors them all from the electrical cabinet. No sensors on the machine, no access permits, no site visits.

See what we monitor →

Detects the faults that cause unplanned downtime

Bearing degradation, cavitation, misalignment, broken rotor bars, winding defects, and mechanical looseness. SAM4 identifies fault type and severity weeks to months before failure.

How ESA detects faults →

Fully managed. Not self-serve.

Every alert is validated by a reliability engineer before it reaches your team. Fault type confirmed, severity assessed, action recommended. No black box.

How our service works →

How it works

Monitor from the MCC. Detect failures months in advance.

SAM4 monitors rotating equipment from the motor control cabinet using Electrical Signature Analysis. Once connected, Samotics manages the full pipeline: signal capture, AI detection, engineer validation, and confirmed maintenance alerts.

1

Connect

SAM4 hardware installs in the MCC or VFD cabinet and captures high-frequency electrical signatures from the motor supply. No sensors on the machine, no wiring to the asset, no work in hazardous or submerged areas. Every connected motor becomes continuously monitored.

2

Detect

AI models analyse the electrical signal to identify deviations from normal operation across the full drivetrain. Every flagged anomaly is reviewed by a Samotics reliability engineer before an alert is issued. No raw alerts reach your team.

3

Act

Your team receives validated diagnostics: identified failure mode, severity assessment, recommended maintenance action, and suggested intervention window. Alerts appear in the SAM4 dashboard and integrate with your CMMS. Maintenance moves from reactive to planned.

Detection lead times typically range from 4 to 20 weeks before failure, depending on the fault type. Deployed on 10,000+ assets across 5 continents.

See the full pipeline →

The science

Mechanical faults leave electrical fingerprints

A worn bearing, a blocked impeller, or a misaligned shaft changes the torque on the motor. That torque variation modulates current and voltage in ways that are invisible to vibration sensors but clearly visible in the electrical spectrum. SAM4 analyzes those spectral signatures from the motor control cabinet, identifying characteristic sideband frequencies that map directly to specific fault types.

Mechanical faults

Bearing degradation
Misalignment
Imbalance
Cavitation

Electrical faults

Broken rotor bars
Winding faults

This is why SAM4 can monitor assets that sensors cannot reach.

See how ESA detects faults →

Proof

Proven across 10,000 monitored assets

Detection rate 95% Of confirmed faults flagged before failure
False positive rate 1.8% After engineer validation of every alert
Validated findings 5,300+ Faults confirmed by on-site inspection
Detection value €80M+ Avoided downtime and repair costs

80+ customers across 5 continents. 10,000+ assets monitored.

“We’ve chosen Samotics as our sole supplier for ESA technology for the coming years because they have shown strong performance in the water industry and are the only company able to deliver this technology at scale.”

— Matt Armitage, Dynamic Maintenance Transformation Lead, Yorkshire Water

Industries

Built for industries with hard-to-reach equipment

Many critical machines operate where sensors cannot be installed. Submerged pumps. Hazardous zones. Remote infrastructure. Sealed process equipment. SAM4 monitors these assets directly from the electrical cabinet.

These industries share the same reliability blind spot: critical rotating equipment that cannot be monitored with traditional sensors.

Water & Wastewater

Submersible pumps and remote pumping stations where vibration sensors cannot be installed. Monitor pump health directly from the MCC.

See water case studies →

Energy Production

Motors operating in ATEX zones and offshore environments where field instrumentation is restricted. Monitor from the MCC without entering hazardous areas.

See energy case studies →

Metals & Mining

Heavy-duty pumps, fans, and conveyors in harsh environments. Detect faults early without installing sensors on inaccessible equipment.

See mining case studies →

Chemicals

Process-critical pumps and compressors in restricted-access areas. Continuous monitoring without shutdowns or ATEX sensor installation.

See chemical case studies →

Additional industries

Airports, pulp & paper, food & beverage, and manufacturing. Plus co-development programmes for new applications.

See all industries →

Trusted by

Strategic partners. Institutional backing. 80+ industrial customers.

SAM4 monitors more than 10,000 rotating assets worldwide.
